title: Narcissus carpetanus
taxonomy: Narcissus carpetanus is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae. It is native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. The plant is a bulbous perennial that produces white, yellow, or sometimes pink flowers. Its classification further categorizes it as being part of the genus Narcissus and the subgenus Pseudonarcissus. The specific classification of Narcissus carpetanus places it within the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Narcissus carpetanus is a species of daffodil that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Traditionally, it was used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, colds, and respiratory issues. The plant's flowers and bulbs are said to have antiviral and antibacterial properties, which may contribute to its medicinal effects. Additionally, the plant's extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Narcissus carpetanus.

side_effects: Narcissus carpetanus, as a plant, is generally considered safe for external use, but there is limited information available on its side effects. Ingesting any part of the plant can be toxic, as it contains toxic compounds such as galantamine. If ingested, it can cause symptoms like nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. intro: Narcissus carpetanus, also known as the Pyrenean daffodil, is a perennial plant belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its trumpet-shaped flowers and slender leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and reduce inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Narcissus carpetanus include alkaloids, saponins, and flavonoids, such as galantamine and crocin, which have been shown to have neuroprotective and antioxidant effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Narcissus carpetanus include tinctures and infusions, often used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
